channel.title: Soup on Bell Favorite Recipes
channel.description: Recent content in Soup on Bell Favorite Recipes

item.title: Tomato Soup Salad
item.description:
Submitted by Emily and Robert Desbois

Friends of the Bride’s Family

Ingredients

  • 1 lemon Jello

  • 1 can tomato soup

  • 1 small cream cheese

  • 3 sticks celery, chopped

  • 1 medium onion, chopped

  • ¾ cup almonds, chopped

  • 1 cup Miracle Whip

Instructions

Heat Jello, soup and cream cheese until melted. Add other ingredients. Put green peppers on top if desired.

Place in fridge. Make a day ahead.

item.title: Tomato Soup Potatoes
item.description:
Submitted by Hazel Pigeau

Grandmother of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 1 lb hamburg
  • 1 medium onion, chopped
  • 5 or 6 medium potatoes, sliced
  • 1 can Heinz tomato soup

Instructions

Brown hamburg and onions. Place potatoes in a greased casserole dish. Add hamburg and onions. Mix in tomato soup.

Cook at 350 degrees for 1 hour, or until potatoes are tender.

item.title: Creamed Potato Soup
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 4 medium round red potatoes, peeled and sliced
  • ½ tsp salt
  • 1 small onion, peeled and sliced
  • 1 clove garlic, minced
  • 4 green onions, coarsely chopped
  • 1 cup skim milk
  • 2 (20 oz) cans no-salt added chicken broth, undiluted
  • 1/8 tsp ground white pepper
  • 1/8 tsp ground nutmeg

Instructions

Combine first 5 ingredients in a 3-quart saucepan; cover and simmer 20 minutes or until potato is tender. Transfer potato mixture in batches to container of an electric blender or food processor; cover and process until smooth. Combine pureed mixture with milk and remaining ingredients. Serve warm or chilled Yield: 9 (3/4 cup) servings.

item.title: Chicken Vegetable Soup
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 3-1/2 cup water
  • 1 cup sliced carrot
  • 1 Tbsp chicken-flavored bouillon granules
  • 1 (8-oz)can mushroom stems and pieces,
  • 1 (14-1/2 oz) can no-salt added whole drained tomatoes, undrained and chopped
  • 1 cup diced zucchini
  • ¼ cup dried minced onion
  • 1 cup cooked chicken breast (skinned before cooking and cooked without salt or fat)
  • 1 tsp dried basil
  • 1 tsp paprika
  • ¾ tsp minced garlic
  • 2 Tbsp dry red wine

Instructions

Combine first 7 ingredients in a Dutch oven. Bring to a boil; cover, reduce heat, and simmer 10 minutes. Add carrot; cover and simmer 10 minutes. Add mushrooms and remaining ingredients; simmer, uncovered, 8 minutes. Yield: 7 (1 cup) servings.

item.title: Broccoli-Cauliflower Soup
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1-1/2 cups water
  • ¼ tsp ground mace
  • 1 (10 oz) pkg frozen chopped broccoli, thawed
  • 1 Tbsp cornstarch
  • 1 (10 oz) pkg frozen cauliflower, thawed
  • 3 cups skim milk, divided
  • 1/3 cup chopped onion
  • ½ tsp salt
  • 2 tsp chicken-flavored bouillon granules
  • 1/8 tsp pepper

Instructions

Combine first 5 ingredients in a Dutch oven; cover and bring to a boil. Cook 5 minutes or until (do not drain); stir in mace. Transfer in batches to container of an electric blender; cover and process until smooth. Return to Dutch oven. Dissolve cornstarch in ½ cup milk; stir into vegetable mixture. Add remaining 2-1/2 cups milk, salt and pepper; cook over medium heat until thickened and bubbly, stirring frequently. Yield: 15 (1/2 cup) servings.

item.title: Tomato Soup Cake
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ cup shortening
  • 1 cup sugar
  • 1 egg
  • 2 cups flour
  • 1 tsp salt
  • 1 tsp cinnamon
  • ½ tsp nutmeg
  • ½ tsp cloves
  • 1 tsp baking soda
  • 1 tin tomato soup
  • 1 cup walnuts
  • 1 cup raisins or dates

Instructions

Cream together shortening and sugar. Fold in beaten egg. Add soup. Mix well. Stir in flour, spices, salt, baking soda, sifted together. Add raisins and nuts last. Bake in moderate oven at 350 degrees for 45 minutes. Ice with cream cheese frosting if desired.

item.title: Vegetable Soup
item.description:
• 3 cups water
  • 2 cups tomato juice
  • 1 large onion, diced
  • 1 cup diced carrots
  • 1 cup diced celery
  • 1 cup diced yellow turnip
  • 1 cup diced peeled potato
  • 1 tsp salt
  • 1/4 tsp pepper

Combine all 9 ingredients in large saucepan. Bring to a boil on medium. Reduce heat. Cover. Simmer for about 20 minutes until vegetables are tender. Makes about 7 cups.

NOTES:

Ingredients

  • The “20 minute simmer” took longer for me. I like my veggies mushy in stuff like this though.
  • Very good, though. An easy 4 out of 5.

Credit to Company’s Coming - The Rookie Cook cookbook.

item.title: Loaded "Baked Potato" Soup
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 Medium baking potatoes, unpeeled
  • 1 can (10 fl oz.) chicken broth
  • 1 cup milk
  • 3 slices of cooked bacon (can be the ready-to-serve microwave stuff)
  • 3/4 cup of shredded cheddar cheese, divided
  • 2 Tbsp sliced green onions, divided
  • 1/4 cup sour cream

Instructions

  1. Pierce potatoes; microwave on HIGH 5 mins., turning over after 2 1/2 mins.  Cut potatoes in chunks.  Combine potatoes, broth, and milk in large microwaveable bowl.  Microwave on HIGH 6 mins., stirring after 3 min.  Slightly crush potatoes with a potato masher.
  2. Reserve 2 Tbsp. each of the bacon and cheese and 1 Tbsp. of the onions for topping; set aside.   Add remaining bacon, cheese, and onions to soup.
  3. Serve topped with reserved bacon, cheese, onions, and sour cream.

item.title: Hamburger Soup (Jenny)
item.description:
• 1.5 lbs. of lean hamburg
  • 3 small onions (chopped fine)

Brown meat and onions, then drain well.

Combine all other ingredients in a large pot with meat and mix well.

Ingredients

  • 1 28 oz. can of tomatoes
  • 3 cans of beef consommé
  • 4 carrots (chopped fine)
  • 1 bay leaf (optional)
  • 1/2 cup of pearl barley
  • pepper to taste
  • 2 cups of water
  • 1 can of tomato soup
  • 3 sticks of celery (chopped fine)
  • 1/2 tsp. of thyme
  • 1/2 tsp. of parsley

Instructions

Simmer covered at least 2 hours or all day if possible.

item.title: Spicy Black Bean Soup
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 Medium onion, finely chopped
  • 4 Garlic Cloves, minced
  • 1/2 Tsp Red Pepper Flakes. (or more if you like it with a bit more zing)
  • 45 oz of canned Black Beans (3 15 oz cans)
  • 14 1/2 oz of chicken broth (or vegetable broth)
  • 1 10 oz can of tomatoes with green chilies (such as Rotel Tomatoes)
  • 1 11 oz can of corn

Instructions

  1. Coat bottom of large stockpot with cooking spray. Add onion and garlic and cook, stirring frequently, until onions are soft but not brown, about 5 minutes.
  2. Place one can of beans in blender; add sautéed onion mixture, and red pepper flakes. Cover and blend on high until smooth, about 30 seconds. Pour mixture back into stockpot.
  3. Place second can of beans and broth in blender and puree until smooth; add to stockpot.
  4. Stir third can of beans (do not puree first), chicken stock, tomatoes and green chilies, and corn into stockpot. Bring to a boil, lower heat to medium and simmer for 20 to 25 minutes. Garnish with sour cream.
  5. Yields about 1 1/2 cups of soup per serving.

Billy’s Notes:I couldn’t find 15 oz cans of black beans, so I just dived all my black beans into thirds, for how the recipe plays out.

item.title: Egg Drop Soup
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 4 cups chicken broth
  • 3 eggs
  • 1/3 cup cold water
  • 3 tbsp corn starch

Instructions

  1. Mix the corn starch slurry (the corn starch and the water) in a bowl
  2. Bring the chicken broth to a simmer in a medium pot
  3. Slowly stir the corn starch slurry into the chicken broth
  4. Let it simmer for around 2-3 mins, stirring occasionally
  5. Whisk the eggs in a separate bowl
  6. Slowly stir the eggs into the soup, while stirring.  The more you stir, the finer the egg “strands” will be.  Think the same technique as making scrambled eggs.  Lots of stirring, little chunks.  Less stirring, big chunks.
  7. Let that simmer for a few mins and enjoy.

Billy’s Note:I put a couple of cups of kimchi in this and it gave it some nice color, texture, and spice.  I’m going to put chicken in it next time to get more protein in it.

item.title: Yankee Pot Roast Soup
item.description:
Ingredients

READY IN:

1hr 45mins

SERVES:

8-10

INGREDIENTS

  • 2 lbsstewing beef, cut in 1 inch cubes (or cut up pot roast)
  • 1 teaspoonsalt
  • 1 teaspoonpepper
  • 1 1⁄2tablespoonsfresh garlic, minced
  • 3 tablespoonsbutter
  • 2 mediumonions, cut in chunks
  • 2 mediumcarrots, cut in thick slices (or I use whole baby carrots)
  • 4 red potatoes, cut in chunks (whatever you consider a decent bite size)
  • 2 stalkscelery, cut in thick slices
  • 1 quartchicken broth(I use the Campbells 900 ml ready-to-serve carton)
  • 1 quartbeef broth
  • 1(156 ml) cantomato paste
  • 2bay leaves
  • 1 teaspoon maggi seasoning sauce or 1 teaspoon bouquet garni liquid seasoning (no clue what this was and didn’t use it)

DIRECTIONS

item.title: Potato Soup (Outback Steakhouse)
item.description:
Outback Steakhouse Potato Soup

Ingredients

  • Potatoes (4 large or up to 8 small potatoes)
  • Kosher Salt
  • Vegetable Oil
  • 2 sticks of butter
  • 1 Onion, diced
  • 3/4 cup of all purpose flour
  • 48 oz of chicken broth
  • 2 cups water
  • Celery salt to taste
  • 1/2 tsp ground black pepper
  • 1/4 tsp dried basil
  • 1/4 tsp sugar
  • 1 1/2 cups heavy cream
  • 4-6 slices of cooked bacon, chopped
  • Chopped chives (depends on your preference)
  • 2 cups shredded Monterrey Jack / Cheddar cheese blend.

Method

Preheat your oven to 350 degrees

item.title: Low Carb Broccoli Cheese Soup
item.description:
• 4 cups broccoli (cut into florets)
  • 3 cups chicken broth (or veggie broth)
  • 3 cups shredded cheddar cheese (a little extra for topping if you wish)
  • 1 cup heavy whipping cream
  • 1/2 package of cream cheese
  • 1 tbsp minced garlic

In a large pot over medium heat, sauté the garlic for about 1 minute or until fragrant (you can add a little butter if you’d like).

Add the chicken broth, heavy cream, and broccoli florets. Bring to a boil, and then reduce the heat to simmer for 10-20 minutes or until the broccoli is tender.

item.title: Keto Soup (The bomb)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 lb Hamburg
  • 1 block Cream Cheese
  • 1 can Hunt’s diced tomatoes
  • 1 box Beef Broth
  • 1/2 Green Pepper
  • 1/4 small onion

Instructions

Brown the hamburg, green pepper, and onion. Whip the cream cheese so it’s nice and smooth. Add the remaining ingredients until cream cheese is melted. Serve.

Billy’s Notes:Jude sent this one to me and said it’s awesome. I still haven’t tried it yet.

item.title: Keto Butternut Squash Soup
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 butternut squash peeled, seeded, cubed
  • 4 cupsbroth
  • 12 oz frozen cauliflower thawed & cooked until soft
  • ½ cup sour cream
  • ¼ cup heavy cream
  • 1 teaspoonsalt

Instant Pot:

Instructions

  1. Put the squash and broth in the instant pot.
  2. Cook on manual high for 15 minutes. You can quick release the pressure at this point or just let it release naturally if you prefer.
  3. Add the cauliflower. Puree with an immersion blender.
  4. Stir in the sour cream and heavy. Season to taste. Serve immediately or let the instant pot stay on warm.

Stovetop:

item.title: Turkey Noodle Soup
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 Tbsp butter
  • 1 small onion, diced
  • 2-3 clove garlic, minced
  • 3-4 carrots, diced
  • 2 stalks celery, chopped
  • 2 Cups cooked turkey, shredded or diced
  • 7 Cups chicken broth
  • 6 oz noodles of your choice
  • 1 tsp dried basil
  • salt and pepper to taste

Instructions

Instructions

  1. In your Instant Pot, combine the onion, garlic, carrot and celery along with the butter.
  2. Push saute and let the veggies cook for 4-5 minutes.
  3. Turn off the saute function, and add the chicken, broth and noodles - place the lid on the Instant Pot and lock, ensure the valve is closed.
  4. Push manual (high) and set the timer for 4 minutes.
  5. The pot will take 8-10 minutes to come to pressure, then will count down the 4 minutes.
  6. Once it beeps, slowly release the pressure and open the lid.
  7. Add salt/pepper and basil to taste.
  8. Serve and enjoy!

This turned out pretty good. I’d give it a solid 4 out 5. I’d even try kicking in a can of diced tomatoes next time to see how that goes too.

item.title: Sam's Roasted Red Pepper & Potato Soup
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 4 red peppers
  • Half a red onion
  • 4 cloves of garlic
  • 2 medium sized potatoes (yellow or white)
  • A carton of vegetable broth
  • Salt & Pepper (to taste)
  • Cayenne pepper (to taste)
  • A gob of butter

Instructions:

Instructions

  1. Turn on your oven broiler to high.
  2. Line a baking sheet with aluminum foil and spray with some cooking spray.
  3. Cut each pepper into fours and line on the baking sheet.
  4. Put in oven and let broil until skins start to turn black.
  5. Once skins have turned black, take out of oven and put into a large mixing bowl.  Place plate on top of it to trap the heat in.  This helps the skin soften so they are easy to remove.  Let the peppers stay in the bowl for 10 minutes.
  6. While you wait, mince your onion and garlic.
  7. Wash and peel potatoes and cut them into cubes.
  8. Once 10 minutes have passed, carefully peel the skins off the peppers.
  9. Once the skin has been peeled off, chop up the peppers.
  10. In a large soup pot, melt butter at the bottom over medium heat.  Combine your onion and garlic.  Sauté for about 2 minutes, until the onions become soft.
  11. Add potatoes and combine for about another minute.
  12. Add in red peppers and stir everything together for about a minute.
  13. Season with salt and pepper.
  14. Empty carton of vegetable broth into the pot, stirring occasionally.
  15. Let soup simmer on low to medium heat, until potatoes are soft.
  16. Carefully sample the broth and add cayenne pepper if needed for an extra bit of spice.
  17. Carefully remove from stovetop and use immersion blender to bend sopu to a velvety texture.
